content.problemStatement:
Appy loves balloons! She wants you to give her balloons on each of NNN consecutive days (numbered 111 through NNN); let's denote the number of balloons Appy wants on day iii by AiA_iAi‚Äč. The problem is that you only have MMM balloons. Fortunately, you can give her candies instead of balloons as well. On each day iii, Appy accepts BiB_iBi‚Äč candies per each balloon you do not give her ‚ÄĒ formally, if you give Appy XiX_iXi‚Äč balloons on day iii, then you have to give her Ci=max(0,Ai‚ąíXi)‚čÖBiC_i = \mathrm{max}(0, A_i - X_i) \cdot B_iCi‚Äč=max(0,Ai‚Äč‚ąíXi‚Äč)‚čÖBi‚Äč candies as well.

Your task is to minimize the maximum number of candies you give Appy on some day ‚ÄĒ find the minimum possible value of max(C1,C2,C3,...,CN)\mathrm{max}(C_1, C_2, C_3, ..., C_N)max(C1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,C3‚Äč,...,CN‚Äč).
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \dots, B_NB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the minimum value of max(C1,C2,C3,...,CN)\mathrm{max}(C_1, C_2, C_3, ..., C_N)max(C1‚Äč,C2‚Äč,C3‚Äč,...,CN‚Äč).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
If you give Appy 0,0,0,1,20, 0, 0, 1, 20,0,0,1,2 balloons on days 111 through 555, then the required numbers of candies on each day are 1,4,9,12,151, 4, 9, 12, 151,4,9,12,15. The maximum number of candies is 151515, required on day 555.
